holdings.infoBox: ONLN is an equity ETF with a total of 21 individual holdings. The top holdings are Amazon.com stock at 22.87%, eBay Inc. at 11.43%, Alibaba Group Holding at 10.86%, Coupang at 5.28%, and Wayfair at 4.73%.

overview.description: The ProShares Online Retail ETF (ONLN) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the ProShares Online Retail index. The fund tracks a global, modified market-cap weighted index of stocks issued by companies classified as online retailers. ONLN was launched on Jul 13, 2018 and is issued by ProShares.

data.title: Retail Sales: Consumer Spending Up for Fourth Straight Month
data.text: According to the Census Bureau's Advance Retail Sales Report, consumer spending climbed for the fourth straight month in May. Headline sales rose 0.9%, almost double the projected 0.5% growth and mark...

data.title: Retail ETFs: Defensive Retail Meets Digital Growth
data.text: The consumer is still spending, but with a higher level of caution. Inflation remains a persistent pressure point, particularly for lower- and middle-income households.

data.title: Online Sales Jump 9.8% as Consumers Lean Into Digital Shopping
data.text: The most revealing number in the latest spate of retail data may not be how much consumers in the United States spent. It may be that 16.9% of all retail sales now happen online.

data.title: Weekly Economic Snapshot: Resilience Amid Record Sentiment Lows
data.text: Last week's economic data was defined by conflicting signals from the consumer. While retail figures suggest resilience, sentiment levels have plummeted to record lows.

data.title: Retail Sales Jump 1.7% in March, Higher Than Expected
data.text: The Advance Retail Sales Report from the Census Bureau showed consumer spending was more robust than expected in March. Headline sales jumped 1.7%, a sharp acceleration from February's 0.7% rise and a...

data.title: Online Holiday Spending Hit Records. AI Helped.
data.text: Use of generative AI chat services for holiday shopping rose nearly 700% from a year earlier.

data.title: Holiday Sales Will Exceed $1 Trillion for the First Time, NRF Predicts
data.text: Retail sales will increase at an annual pace of between 3.7% and 4.2% in the period ranging Nov. 1 through Dec. 31, the trade group projects.

data.title: US holiday sales set to top $1 trillion for the first time, NRF forecasts
data.text: U.S. holiday sales are expected to surpass $1 trillion for the first time, the National Retail Federation forecast on Thursday.

data.title: Amazon stock hits record, lights up ETFs
data.text: Amazon's cloud business knocked it out of the park and CEO Andy Jassy hinted that the unit is gaining momentum heading into the next few months giving him 'significant confidence' he said.

data.title: US Consumers Spend ‘Robustly' After Almost $1 Trillion Holiday
data.text: After a nearly $1 trillion holiday season, U.S. consumer spending shows no sign of slowing. The exact figure is $994 billion, according to recent findings from the National Retail Federation.

data.title: Online spending grew 3% to a record $1.2T over holiday period, says Salesforce
data.text: Online spending rose 3% to a record $1.2 trillion globally over the holiday period, with U.S.-sales, specifically, growing 4% to $282 billion.

data.title: Salesforce: 28% Uptick in Returns Could Dampen Record Holiday Spending
data.text: Artificial intelligence (AI) helped holiday sales reach record levels this year, new Salesforce data shows. However, the company said in a report issued Monday (Jan. 6), high rates of returns could hi...

data.title: Holiday sales jump more than expected as retailers slash prices
data.text: Heading into the holiday season, many US retailers described their consumers as "selective," "cautious" and "conservative," and making "needs-based" purchases.

data.title: US consumers spent record $13.3B on Cyber Monday

data.title: Cyber Monday sales expected to reach record $13.2B: Adobe
data.text: Cyber Weekend sales have transitioned into Cyber Monday, with Adobe Analytics expecting consumers to spend as much as $13.2 billion on this day alone after spending $10.8 billion on Black Friday. This...

data.title: Black Friday spending in US retail stores muted compared to online purchases
data.text: Sales at brick-and-mortar stores grew just 0.7% year-over-year, according to preliminary estimates by payments processor Mastercard, and were lower according to data firm Facteus.

data.title: Black Friday online spending hits $10.8B, fueled by discounts and mobile shopping
data.text: US shoppers spent a record $10.8 billion online this Black Friday, marking a 10.2% year-over-year increase, according to Adobe Analytics. As the official kickoff to the holiday shopping season, Black ...

data.title: Black Friday data shows US shoppers spent $10.8 billion online
data.text: It was a 10% rise from a year ago, according to Adobe Analytics.

data.title: Online Black Friday spending is on track to break a record this year, despite inflation
data.text: Adobe reports record $7.9 billion in online spending on Black Friday so far, up 8.2% from 2023. Adobe expects final online Black Friday numbers to be between $10.7 and $11 billion.
